hero_scroll_sequence.overview: Scroll-driven 3-phase hero: portrait visible → zoom into forehead → vertical split reveals blog content beneath. Creates the illusion of entering the subject's mind.
hero_scroll_sequence.scroll_container: The hero section height is 300vh (3x viewport). The viewport pins the hero (position: sticky, top: 0, h-screen) while the user scrolls through the 300vh wrapper. scrollYProgress 0→1 maps across the full 300vh.

phase_1_initial.description: Page load state. Portrait fully visible, entry animations play.

phase_2_zoom.description: Image progressively zooms into the top of the head (forehead/crown area). Name fades out. Nav fades out. Vignettes tighten. The feeling is being pulled into the person's mind.

image_zoom.note: 20% from top = forehead area. Scale origin stays fixed so zoom targets the head, not center of frame.

name_fadeout.note: Name exits early so it's gone well before the split. Slides up + blurs as it fades.

vignette_intensify.note: Vignettes close in slightly, then fade as image fills the frame.

bottom_gradient_fadeout.note: The bottom fade-to-cream disappears since we're zooming past it.

phase_3_split.description: The zoomed image splits vertically down the center. Left half slides left, right half slides right. Clean cut. Blog content is revealed beneath as the halves separate.

implementation.technique: Two identical images with clipPath. Left half clips to left 50%, right half clips to right 50%. On split, each slides outward.
implementation.note: At scroll 40%, replace the single image with two clipped copies (or switch clipPath on a single image to two layers). This must be seamless — no visible pop.

left_half.note: Slides fully off-screen left. 105% ensures no sub-pixel edge visible.

split_edge_shadow.description: Subtle dark shadow on the inner edges of each half as they separate, creating depth. Fades in at 42%, gone by 65%.
split_edge_shadow.value: inset 4px 0 20px rgba(0,0,0,0.4) on right edge of left half, inset -4px 0 20px rgba(0,0,0,0.4) on left edge of right half

background_reveal.note: The blog content section fades in as the gap between halves widens. Starts slightly after split begins so there's a moment of dark void first.

remaining_overlays_fadeout.description: Any remaining vignettes, grain fade to 0.

phase_4_settled.description: Image halves are fully off-screen. Blog content is fully visible. Nav reappears. Scroll continues naturally into blog content below.

nav_reappear.note: Nav returns but now over the blog content, not the hero image. Could transition to a solid/frosted bg since it's over light content now.

blog_content.state: Fully visible, interactive, normal scroll behavior resumes.

motion_values_to_css.note: filter property needs template string, not raw number

dom_structure.description: How the hero HTML should be structured to support the split
structure: div.image-left (absolute inset-0, clipPath inset(0 50% 0 0), will-change: transform)
next/image (fill, priority, objectFit cover, objectPosition center 20%, scale driven by scroll)
div.image-right (absolute inset-0, clipPath inset(0 0 0 50%), will-change: transform)
next/image (fill, same as left — these are two copies of same image)
div.vignettes (left, right, top, bottom gradients — same as current)
div.blog-content-preview (the content revealed behind the split, opacity animated)
nav.floating-nav (fixed, z-50, opacity driven by scroll)

performance.will_change: Apply to both image halves and nav: will-change: transform, opacity
performance.gpu_acceleration: Use translate3d(x, 0, 0) not translateX for GPU compositing
performance.image_copies: Two next/image elements with same src — browser deduplicates the download, just renders twice with different clip paths
performance.overflow: hero-sticky must have overflow: hidden to prevent image halves bleeding during slide
performance.pointer_events: All decorative overlays pointer-events-none. Nav pointer-events toggled with opacity.

entry_animations_unchanged.note: The initial load animations from the current hero stay the same: per-letter name reveal with stagger, Framer Motion. These play on mount, before any scrolling happens. The scroll sequence begins after.
